question: Suppose we have an urn with 4 red and 7 green balls. We choose two balls without replacement. Let A = {first ball is red} and B = {second ball is green}. Are A and B independent? Answer Independent or Dependent.

conversation.content: Suppose we have an urn with 4 red and 7 green balls. We choose two balls without replacement. Let A = {first ball is red} and B = {second ball is green}. Are A and B independent? Answer Independent or Dependent.

P(A)=4/11. P(B)=P(B|A)P(A)+P(B|A^c)P(A^c) = (7/10)(4/11)+(6/10)(7/11)=7/11. P(A∩B)=P(A)P(B|A)=(4/11)(7/10)=14/55, which is not equal to P(A)P(B)=(4/11)(7/11). So A and B are dependent.
then the answer: Dependent

then the answer: Dependent
